## World Coherence Evaluation

**Score: 9/10**

This world bible demonstrates exceptional coherence across all five dimensions. The setting's foundational logic (geological flooding) propagates consistently through economics, politics, culture, and magic. Elements that could feel arbitrary—blood oaths, salt currency, the monastery's power—all emerge organically from environmental conditions. The magic system features explicit rules with concrete costs, while the world's specificity ensures it serves story rather than existing as decorative background. The interconnected conspiracy gives every element narrative purpose.

**Supporting Passages:**

> "The region's economy runs on three classes of labor: Freemen who pay taxes to the Council and fees to the Covenant for 'protection'; Indentured workers bonded to specific employers; and the Tithed, Covenant workers who work the most dangerous jobs—tideglass diving, body disposal, interrogation assistance—with a two-year life expectancy."

This passage exemplifies how geography, economy, and politics interlock. The dangerous tideglass diving exists because of the geology; the indentured labor system exists because of economic stratification from the flooding; and the Covenant's power exists because they control the most valuable resource and the most desperate workers.

> "**The Iron Vulnerability**: Iron disrupts blood magic by interfering with essence flow. Wounds inflicted by iron weapons heal slower; iron chains prevent Bloodwrights from using their abilities. Many Covenant operatives carry iron daggers specifically to disable opposing Bloodwrights."

A magic system vulnerability that's specific, has clear tactical implications, and creates interesting combat dynamics. This isn't a soft limitation but a concrete rule with worldbuilding consequences—iron becomes strategically valuable beyond its mundane utility.

> "The faithful believe: The sea's advance is a test, not a punishment; The dead become part of the Drowned God's domain, returned to the cycle; Those who die by drowning are especially blessed, as they're the first to be 'released'."

Cultural
